mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits into lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month when policy is actively easing. The signal is confirmed when the policy rate is declining on a 1-3 month basis and fails if rate cuts occur during recessionary stress that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods when FEDFUNDS falls over the prior 1-3 months, or if the hit rate is below 55% across triggered easing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply above its recent trend, labor-market deterioration signals weaker household income and rising recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect is strongest when the unemployment rate breaks higher by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months and fails if unemployment rises without concurrent cyclical slowing in activity.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average 20d return after triggers is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening nominal demand and payroll-supported consumption, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This mechanism requires both sales and employment to improve together; a rise in one without the other is not sufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60d SPX returns, or if combined-strength episodes show negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and firm-level earnings leverage, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over roughly one month. The signal fails when industrial production growth is a one-off rebound after a prior collapse and does not persist for at least two monthly observations.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO upswings are followed by nonpositive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the Rank IC is below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior surge signals weakening construction demand and cooling private investment, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism activates when housing starts fall meaningfully from their recent peak and fails if the decline is offset by strong labor-market or retail-demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST drawdowns are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or if the conditional mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS is not increasing commensurately, real policy rates fall and equity valuations improve over the following month. The signal is strongest when CPI inflation accelerates above its recent 6-month pace and fails if inflation rises alongside aggressive rate hikes that tighten financial conditions.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rises above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS is flat
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction between rising CPIAUCSL and flat FEDFUNDS does not produce positive excess 20d SPX returns, or if the sign reverses when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment environment combined with continued payroll gains indicates resilient consumer income and lower near-term recession risk, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This mechanism requires both UNRATE near cycle lows and PAYEMS still rising; it fails if labor tightness is already peaking and payroll growth is decelerating.
regime_hint: late-cycle but still expanding labor market
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE plus rising-PAYEMS episodes do not outperform baseline on 60d SPX returns, or if the sign becomes negative when payroll growth slows.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, weak retail sales alongside weakening housing starts should identify an impending demand slowdown and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The joint deterioration matters more than either series alone, and the signal fails outside tightening regimes or when one demand proxy improves materially.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weakness of RSAFS and HOUST does not predict negative 20d SPX returns specifically during tightening cycles, or if returns are positive in most triggered cases.
